program_chi: 雙渠道雙語研究文學士
learning_outcomes:
BMBL 1. Major
Programme: Upon
completing the programme, students will have: 1. Achieved a new understanding of how knowledge of language is
organized and transmitted through the visual-spatial modality, as opposed to
spoken languages, organized and transmitted in fundamentally different ways; 2. Appreciated how this new understanding can potentially
transform the society’s perception about sign language, giving it a linguistic status that is equal
to spoken languages; 3. To be able to evaluate critically
the development of bimodal bilingualism in society from the perspective of
information accessibility and effective communication in domains of
education, social and public communication, child care, counselling and
clinical services; and 4. To be proficient in spoken languages and sign languages in
order to undertake responsibilities in professional settings which call for
such linguistic skills.
study_scheme:
Bimodal Bilingual Studies Bimodal Bilingual Studies Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major
Programme Requirement (for Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders)
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 57 units of courses as follows:

and 4102 (capstone courses)

and 4202 (capstone courses)

Explanatory
Note:
1.  |  BMBL,
CHED, EDUC, HKSL, LING, SLPA, SOWK and TRAN courses at 2000 and above
level as included in the Major Programme Requirement will be included in
the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  For
students whose native language is Hong Kong Sign Language (HKSL) or who
possess equivalent competence in HKSL, subject to the approval of the
Major Department, they should take course(s) of a language other than
English, Chinese and HKSL, and LING courses in place of the HKSL courses,
as specified below:

6 units  |  6 units

9 units
and above  |  at

and HKSL, and LING courses at 2000 or above level
